blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a gem. The owner is genuinely welcoming and takes pride in what they do. If you've never had pho before, this is a great place to start. Reviewers say it's made the authentic way and you can taste the difference.

The pho is the thing to order here. It's the kind of bowl that actually satisfies, not just a quick bite. The price is easy on the wallet too, which makes it perfect for a casual weeknight dinner or a quick lunch when you need something warm and filling.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which is super convenient.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is worth knowing about, especially if you have kids who need a post-activity treat or you're looking for something lighter than the usual options around here. It's a Brazilian-style aÃ§aÃ­ shop and the vibe is cozy and friendly. The staff is genuinely welcoming, not just going through the motions.

The aÃ§aÃ­ is self-serve, so you build your own bowl with whatever toppings you want. They also have crepes and coxinha, which is a Brazilian fried chicken snack that's worth trying if you've never had one. It's a fun place to poke around and try something different.

They're closed on Tuesdays and open until 10pm most other nights, which makes it a solid option for an after-dinner outing.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
If you need a place for a special occasion and you want it to actually feel special, Ray's on the River delivers. It sits right on the Chattahoochee over in Sandy Springs, and the setting is the kind of thing that impresses out-of-town guests without you having to explain why it's a big deal. Think anniversary dinners, milestone birthdays, or a date night when you want to do it right.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, so expect things done in a more refined, Southern-influenced style. It's the kind of place where the food matches the view. Prices are on the higher end, so this isn't an every-week spot, but for when you need it, it earns its reputation.

Sunday hours are split, with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so plan around that if you're going on a Sunday evening.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to for a lot of people in this area for a while now, and the food from the kitchen holds up. It's a solid pick for a casual family dinner or lunch with coworkers. Open every day and late enough to work even if you're coming from a long evening.

The food is the reason people keep going back. It's straightforward Mexican done well and priced fairly. Where longtime regulars have noticed a change is at the bar. Margarita prices have crept up quite a bit compared to other spots nearby, so just be aware if you're planning on a few rounds.

Go for the food and you won't be let down. Just maybe check the drink menu before you order a round.

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a well-known name around Atlanta and worth the short drive down to Akers Mill. It's a tiny place with one communal table, so don't go expecting a sit-down dinner experience. Think of it more like: get in, get your food, and enjoy every bite. Great for a casual lunch or an early dinner when you just want really good barbecue.

What makes it different from most BBQ spots is a Korean influence that shows up in the flavors. It's still very much barbecue, but there's a depth to the seasoning that keeps people talking about it. The crowds are real, so going closer to when they open is a smart move.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday and close at 8pm, so this takes a little planning. They're also closed Sunday and Monday.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is the kind of neighborhood Chinese spot that has something for everyone on the menu. It's casual and easy, good for a weeknight dinner when you want something filling without a lot of fuss. The menu goes beyond typical takeout fare, with options like hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce if you're feeling adventurous.

The BBQ duck and hot pot are the things that set this place apart from a standard Chinese takeout spot. It's a good call for a family dinner or when you want to try something a little different without driving far.

They're open until 10pm every night, which makes it a solid option when dinner plans come together late.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside is one of those places people around here keep coming back to, and for good reason. It's a casual, friendly spot that works for a family dinner, a date night, or just grabbing a pizza when you don't feel like cooking. The ratings speak for themselves.

The Hot Honey Char pizza is a must-try, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are people's favorite way to start the meal. The Caesar Salad is also worth ordering if you want something fresh and simple on the side. The wings apparently go so well with the pizza that people use the leftover sauce for dipping their crust.

They're closed on Mondays, so keep that in mind for your next pizza night.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've been wanting to try Indian food or you're already a fan, Royal Spice on Hammond Drive is worth a visit. The staff is friendly and genuinely helpful about explaining the menu, which is great if you're not sure where to start. The vibe is relaxed with quiet background music, nothing loud or rushed.

The entrees and appetizers both get high marks from regulars. The servers are good about walking you through the options and helping you figure out what to order based on your taste. It works well for a sit-down dinner with family or a nice casual meal with friends.

Note they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, reopening at 4:30pm. Don't show up mid-afternoon expecting to get a table.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
